tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: What type of mutation is shown in the image?

dialogs.thought: Orient to the figure type (e.g., sequencing chromatograms) and labeled tissue compartments to infer mutation type.

content.content: Three side-by-side Sanger sequencing chromatogram panels with base calls at the top and arrows pointing to a nucleotide position. Panels are labeled at the bottom: "SCC", "Dermis", and "Epidermis". The SCC panel shows a mixed peak at the arrowed base compared with the other panels.

dialogs.thought: Read any labels/base calls to identify the specific nucleotide change and context (SCC vs dermis/epidermis).

arguments.attribute: sequencing peak pattern at the arrowed nucleotide (evidence of heteroplasmy/mixed base)

dialogs.thought: Confirm that the SCC chromatogram shows mixed peaks at the arrowed site consistent with heteroplasmy, compared with non-tumor tissue.

content.content: In the SCC panel at the arrowed position there are overlapping peaks (mixed signal) rather than a single clean peak, consistent with a heteroplasmic/mixed-base call (shown as 'N' in the base string).

dialogs.content: The image shows a somatic heteroplasmic mutation in the skin mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) from a patient with squamous cell carcinoma (SCC). The mutation is a C to T transition at nucleotide position 438 in the noncoding D-loop region.

whitelist: The image shows a somatic heteroplasmic mutation in the skin mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) from a patient with squamous cell carcinoma (SCC). The mutation is a C to T transition at nucleotide position 438 in the noncoding D-loop region.
